metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1746
metadata.comment: Rationale: Researchers investigate the effect of yotiao on NMDA-mediated currents by performing whole-cell recordings of transfected HEK 293 cells over expressing NMDA (NR1A-containing) receptors alone or together with yotiao. NMDA currents were enhanced by application of a cAMP analog, and these were greater in the presence of yotiao. Since yotiao anchors the type I protein phosphatase (PP1), investigators also assessed whether the regulation of NMDA current by yotiao might involve PP1 activity. By pharmacologically blocking PP1 activity, authors show that tonic PP1 activity associated with yotiao negatively affects NMDA receptor-mediated current.. Experimental description: Reference 10: To construct a yotiao-GFP expression vector, we generated the entire coding region (nucleotides 131 to 5169) by re- verse transcription of human brain polyadenylated RNA with an oligonucleotide to the 3' end of yotiao.
